title: Add 'Add to Home Screen' button for PWA installation

requirements.summary: Add Web App Manifest file with MediFind branding and soft green theme to enable PWA installability
acceptanceCriteria: A manifest.json file exists in frontend/public with name, short_name, theme_color, background_color, display: standalone, and icons fields
The manifest is linked in frontend/index.html via a <link rel="manifest"> tag
The app theme-color meta tag is set to match the soft green palette

file_operations.description: Create Web App Manifest with name 'MediFind', short_name 'MediFind', theme_color matching the soft green palette (#10b981 or equivalent from the OKLCH color scheme), background_color '#ffffff', display 'standalone', start_url '/', scope '/', and icons array referencing the generated PWA icons at frontend/public/assets/generated/medifind-icon.dim_192x192.png and frontend/public/assets/generated/medifind-icon.dim_512x512.png with appropriate sizes and types.
Wire the generated 192x192 PWA icon (flat soft green medical cross or leaf on white background) into the manifest as a static asset for standard icon size.
Wire the generated 512x512 PWA icon (flat soft green medical cross or leaf on white background) into the manifest as a static asset for high-resolution displays.

Add <link rel="manifest" href="/manifest.json"> in the <head> section to link the manifest file. Add or update the theme-color meta tag to match the soft green palette (e.g., <meta name="theme-color" content="#10b981">). Ensure the title remains 'MediFind'.

requirements.summary: Add visible 'Add to Home Screen' button that triggers native install prompt on supported browsers and shows iOS fallback instructions
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Add to Home Screen' button or banner is rendered in the UI when the browser supports the install prompt
Clicking the button triggers the native browser PWA install prompt
The button/banner is hidden after the prompt is accepted or dismissed
On iOS Safari, a fallback instructional message is shown instead (e.g., 'Tap the Share icon then Add to Home Screen')
The button/banner uses the existing soft green color palette and matches the app's visual theme

file_operations.description: Create a custom hook that listens for the 'beforeinstallprompt' event, stores the event object in state, provides a function to trigger the prompt, and detects iOS Safari to return an isIOS flag. Return { deferredPrompt, promptInstall, isInstalled, isIOS } where promptInstall calls prompt() on the deferred event and hides the UI after user choice.

Create a component that uses the useInstallPrompt hook to render a dismissible banner or button. On non-iOS browsers with beforeinstallprompt support, show a button with soft green styling that calls promptInstall on click. On iOS, show a small instructional banner with text like 'Tap the Share icon, then Add to Home Screen' with an appropriate icon. Include a dismiss button that hides the prompt. Use Tailwind classes matching the existing soft green theme.

Import and render the InstallPrompt component below the header (inside the Layout component, after the header but before the main content slot) so it appears as a dismissible banner across all pages. Ensure the Layout remains visually consistent with the existing sticky header and footer structure.

requirements.summary: Register a minimal service worker to cache the app shell and meet PWA installability requirements
acceptanceCriteria: A sw.js service worker file exists in frontend/public
The service worker is registered in frontend/src/main.tsx or frontend/index.html
The app passes Chrome's PWA installability criteria (manifest + service worker)
The service worker does not break existing app functionality

file_operations.description: Create a minimal service worker that handles the 'install' event by opening a cache (e.g., 'medifind-v1') and caching the root URL ('/') and the main HTML file. Handle the 'activate' event to clean up old caches. Use a simple fetch strategy that attempts network first and falls back to cache for basic offline support without aggressive caching.

Add a <script> block in the <head> or at the end of <body> that registers the service worker at /sw.js using navigator.serviceWorker.register('/sw.js') wrapped in a feature detection check (if ('serviceWorker' in navigator)). Handle registration success and error silently or log to console. This avoids modifying main.tsx per the constraint.
